title: Causal race-state baseline feature block
summary: Build a compact baseline around current-row race phase, estimated remaining distance, compound class, tyre-age ratios, degradation transforms, and position-change indicators while preserving all raw columns.
rationale: The attached run repeatedly showed that raw features alone were weaker than deterministic race/stint context, and raw-data inspection showed complete columns with shared train/test categories. A row-preserving causal baseline gives later experiments a stable anchor without target-derived or future-lap features.
implementation_hint: Use only current-row columns to derive estimated total laps, laps remaining, race-phase flags, compound ordinal and slick/wet flags, TyreLife ratios, positive/absolute LapTime_Delta and degradation, and signed position-change flags. Train a bounded LightGBM or CatBoost baseline and keep this feature block as the ablation reference.
expected_effect: Medium expected ROC AUC improvement over a raw-only tree model; confidence high as a robust baseline rather than a final differentiator.
risk: Estimated total laps can be unstable on anomalous RaceProgress rows. Clamp implausible estimates and avoid per-race aggregates that use future laps within the same sequence.
